title: CapSched overlay traceability row schema

row_shape.relation: documents | observes | anchors | checks | models | refines | blocks | falsifies | supersedes | forbidden_upgrade
row_shape.evidence_class: analysis_only | source_only | observation_only | model_checked | build_only | counterexample | implementation_plan | code_patch | protection_evidenced

linux_anchors.anchor_class: current_source_anchor | future_gap | trace_catalog_plan | public_abi | private_helper | patch_anchor
linux_anchors.anchor_kind: file | function | struct | field | tracepoint | pattern | config | makefile | gap
linux_anchors.drift_status: ok | path_changed | symbol_missing | pattern_missing | semantic_recheck_required | gap | deprecated | unknown

hard_rules: N ids are chronological work ids, not semantic ids.
N ids must not directly support CLAIM ids.
source_only, observation_only, build_only, and model_checked rows do not imply protection_evidenced.
LINUX anchors default to authority_claim=false, monitor_verified=false, and protection_claim=false.
A missing Linux anchor is a gap, not proof that the semantic obligation is unnecessary.
When upstream source changes, drift_status must be rechecked before using the row for design decisions.
